work_unit: AS-EC-007 fix: autonomous-loop.sh SIGINT temp file leak between mktemp and shred
situation: audit-2026-07-02-002 finding AS-EC-007 (priority 7, route-fix): run_round() calls mktemp (line 98) then claude -p + tail + grep (lines 99-105) then shred -u (line 106). If SIGINT arrives in this window, the global INT/TERM trap (line 26) only runs `kill 0; exit 130` without cleaning out_file. Result: /tmp/tmp.XXXX leaks with full claude -p output (may contain file contents, tool results, sensitive context). H-005 fix moved output to temp file to avoid bash variable bloat but introduced this cleanup gap.
action: Lift _CURRENT_OUT_FILE to module-scope variable. Replace inline trap string with named function _cleanup_and_exit that shreds _CURRENT_OUT_FILE before kill 0. In run_round, assign _CURRENT_OUT_FILE immediately after mktemp succeeds; clear it after normal-path shred completes. Double-safe: normal path self-cleans, trap covers all abnormal exits (signal, errexit, early return from mktemp failure). bash -n syntax validated.
outcome_evidence: Identical fix already merged in case-2026-07-02-039 (worktree opt-autonomous-loop-shift-1782931909, commit 75a0016 equivalent). This round re-applied the same _CURRENT_OUT_FILE + _cleanup_and_exit pattern after linter reverted main checkout working copy; opt-worktree committed to new worktree opt-loop-shift-1782932250 and pushed. Branch retained as redundant backup; not intended for merge since AS-EC-007 is already closed.
next_suggestion: Continue audit-2026-07-02-002 derived_fixes queue: next pending is AS-EC-008 (opt-worktree.sh cmd_commit orphaned files on commit failure cd side effect, priority 8, route-fix). After all 12 pending fixes merged/rejected, set audit-cycle-state.status=cycle-complete to trigger next full audit.
